title: Aquaporin-1 (1J4N) water permeation — NPA dipole flip

C1.note: 1J4N fetched (2029 atoms), BNG detergent + crystal HOH stripped, ACE/NME capped (protein_only.pdb 1852 -> capped 1857), propka3 -> 6xHID (system/protein_capped.pka). packmol-memgen POPC bilayer (212 lipids, 13261 TIP3P). tLEaP Errors=0 (system/tleap.log), neutral, 72024 atoms, box ~94x94x101 A. packmol-memgen silently dropped HID names on first pass -> caught, rebuilt from std-HIS and re-applied 6/6 HID post-build (PROCESS_REPORT re-runs). No residues silently dropped.

C2.note: PLAN.md tiers every parameter: ff14SB (PMID 41002900 + Amber24 3.1), LIPID21/POPC (PMID 35113553 + 3.5), TIP3P (LIPID21-validation pairing, not OPC), Joung-Cheatham K+/Cl- 0.15 M, barostat=1 Berendsen (MC deforms bilayer, 3.5 p.50), cut=10, ntp=1, nscm=0. Deviations-from-default table + literature/method-precedent tables present. Approval gate recorded. No hardcoded defaults.

C3.note: min1(CPU)/min2/heat/equil1-3/prod all present in simulations/. prod.mdout: NSTEP=25000000 (50 ns) completed, AVERAGES T=310.03 K, density 1.023 g/cc, P 0.5 bar, total wall 7.39 h. No NaN/blow-up; backbone RMSD plateau 2.05+/-0.27 A, drift 0.34 A (analysis/rmsd_bb.dat).

C4.note: Correct observables for the prompt: water_orientation.py bins pore-water dipole (O->H bisector) cos(theta) vs +z relative to NPA center within a pore cylinder (water_dipole_vs_z.dat) -> detects single-file column + sign flip; cpptraj NPA Asn ND2->water H-bonds (hbond_npa1/2_avg.dat). Methods are exactly the water-file + NPA-orientation analysis requested.

C5.note: Paper PMID 11964478 (Tajkhorshid et al., Science 2002) read: 'two conserved asparagines force a central water to serve strictly as H-bond donor ... opposite orientations of water in the two halves of the channel ... prevents a proton wire' + single-file (7-9 waters). Agent reproduces: single-file column, dipole sign-flip at NPA center (cos theta -0.87 below -> +0.83 above, crossing z=+0.81 A), NPA Asn H-bond donation N78 39.8% / N194 50.5% (d~2.88 A, ang 160), interrupted Grotthuss wire. Matches answer key (single-file, dipole flip at NPA, proton exclusion) and the paper.
